title: Isotrema molle
taxonomy: Isotrema molle is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Isotrema, which is a group of evergreen shrubs or small trees. The family Apocynaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. Within this family, Isotrema molle is likely to be part of the subfamily Rauvolfioideae or Plumerioideae. Further research may be needed to determine the exact classification of Isotrema molle within its family and genus.

intro: Isotrema molle, also known as soft comfrey, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal and nutritional properties, and is valued for its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support bone health due to its rich content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The bioactive constituents of Isotrema molle include mucilages, flavonoids, and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Soft comfrey is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions to support tissue repair, reduce joint pain, and promote overall well-being.
